title: Vibes Design System
summary: freee's Vibes design system for Japanese financial-product web applications, covering React/CSS components, Japanese label conventions, high-density data layouts, and contribution material. The official repository is unarchived but its last observed code push was 2024-08-01, so it is implementation-history evidence rather than a current production baseline.
lessons: Use for high-density financial workflow patterns, Japanese label/data formatting, and disclosure and error-recovery case studies specific to accounting and business software.

Treat as implementation-history evidence: study interaction patterns and accessibility decisions from the period of active development, but verify every implementation claim against current web standards before adoption.
caveats: Repository (freee/vibes) last pushed 2024-08-01; treat all implementation details (APIs, dependencies, component behaviour) as potentially outdated — revalidate every claim before adopting any pattern.
Do not adopt Vibes component APIs, CSS variables, or token names as current AwesomeDS stack; extract decision rationale and UX patterns only.
Revalidate this record if the repository resumes active development.
licenseNote: Public first-party freee documentation and repository; cite and synthesize.
antiImitationNote: Extract Japanese financial UX patterns and high-density layout decisions; do not import Vibes component APIs or freee brand identity.
